exemplar.title: How does the mass of volatile organic compounds extracted from books by a distillation differ amongst books of different ages (24, 34, 44, 54, 64 years old) when 3 grams of paper from each book are left in 150cm3 of acetone for 15 days?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the mass of volatile organic compounds extracted from books by a distillation differ amongst books of different ages (24, 34, 44, 54, 64 years old) when 3 grams of paper from each book are left in 150cm3 of acetone for 15 days?

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and purposefully with clear, engaging background context
Research question is precisely stated and tightly linked to later discussion
Methodology is thorough, includes controlled variables, safety considerations, and iterative trials
weaknesses: Some controls (paper composition, solvent selectivity) could be more rigorous
Typographical error in listing book ages could cause confusion
Ventilation/fume-hood usage for VOC handling is mentioned only briefly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the top band (5â6), and although the methodology has minor weaknesses, it is complete with effective source selection and control design. This justifies the full 6 marks for Focus and Method.

strengths: Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, with effective use of chemical concepts and diagrams
Terminology is accurate and used consistently across sections
Source material from journals and technical reports is applied appropriately to support background claims
weaknesses: Application of source material within the analysis is somewhat uneven and could be more deeply integrated
Mechanistic details (e.g. glycosidic bond cleavage) are described without full reaction equations
Quantitative comparison to published VOC data is minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With two strands solidly in the 5â6 range (subject knowledge and terminology) and one strand in 3â4 (application of sources), the overall performance sits at the upper half of the 3â4 band. A score of 5 reflects clear understanding tempered by uneven source integration.

strengths: Research draws on substantial primary data (ten trials per age) and a strong literature foundation
Analysis uses averages, standard deviations, RÂ² and uncertainty tables to support clear conclusions
Discussion critically evaluates limitations and proposes concrete improvements (same-publisher books, GCâMS)
weaknesses: No inferential statistical tests (e.g. t-tests) to assess significance of observed trends
Health-risk discussion is superficial; lacks quantitative exposure limits
Further quantitative linkage (e.g. ClausiusâClapeyron equation) is suggested but not implemented
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigationâs research quality clearly falls into the 7â9 band and analysis/discussion largely in 4â6. Overall critical thinking performance is strong enough to merit placement in the mid-range of the 7â9 band, equating to 8 marks.

strengths: Structure follows IB conventions (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, results, conclusion, references, appendices)
Layout is consistent, with numbered pages, labelled tables/figures, and word count displayed
weaknesses: Some figure captions lack full bibliographic details
Dense paragraphs in procedures could be converted to numbered steps for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both presentation strands meet the highest band (3â4): the reportâs organization and formatting consistently adhere to IB requirements. Minor omissions do not detract from awarding the full 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows IB EE conventions: title page, contents, introduction, methodology, results, conclusion, references and appendices. Headings are logical, facilitating navigation; only the abstract is missing, but structure overall is **clearly appropriate**.

The student provides a clear, engaging explanation of bibliosmia, outlines the chemical processes involved and consistently links these to the investigationâs purpose. The distinction between old and new book odours is articulated with specific chemistry examples (lignin degradation, VOC generation), demonstrating **accurate and purposeful communication**. The section titles and background context make the focus evident from the outset.

Analysis includes averaging, standard deviations, error bars and RÂ² calculation, plus uncertainty tables. Nonetheless, statistical treatment is basic (no inferential tests), and conclusions about âsharp increasesâ are not backed by significance testing. Thus, depth is **clear and supported, but not excellent**.

The student consults a respectable body of literature and designs ten-trial data sets for each age, generating a substantial volume of primary data that is **mostly relevant to the question**. Gaps such as uncontrolled paper composition prevent a higher band.

The background demonstrates solid chemical understanding (hydrolysis, electrophilic substitution, intermolecular forces) and links these concepts to VOC formation. Explanations are coherent and supported by diagrams and references, showing **clear, accurate subject knowledge with effective source use**, though not at a publishable depth.

Chemical terminology (VOCs, lignin hydrocracking, STDEV, RÂ²) is employed accurately and consistently across sections. Occasional minor phrasing issues do not impede understanding, meeting the descriptor for **accurate and consistent terminology usage**.

Methodology is described step-by-step, includes controlled variables, safety and preliminary trials, indicating informed choices. However, the method relies on a crude mass-difference approach without rigorous identification/quantification of toluene, and some controls (paper composition, solvent selectivity) are weak. Range of sources is acceptable but not extensive in justifying each procedural choice, yielding **mostly complete but not fully sophisticated methodology**.

Pagination, labelled tables/figures, and consistent formatting are present; word count and title are stated. Minor inconsistencies in table alignment and occasional image blurring exist, yet layout still meets IB expectations, meriting the **higher presentation band**.

The discussion links results to chemical theory and compares with literature; evaluation identifies multiple limitations and proposes concrete improvements (same-publisher books, GCâMS). The critique is thoughtful but not fully penetrating (e.g., solvent selectivity implications are noted but not quantified), giving a **good, partially critical evaluation**.

Literature from journals and technical reports is cited throughout background and evaluation, and specific studies (Gibson 2012, Strlic 2009) are applied to support claims. Still, source use within the analysis section is limited; quantitative comparison to published data is minimal, so application is **mostly relevant and appropriate but not consistently integrated**.

A precise question is stated in measurable terms (mass of VOCs from 3 g paper / 150 cmÂ³ acetone over 15 days for five defined ages). The wording is narrow enough to guide data collection and directly underpins later discussion, showing **excellent focus and alignment** with the investigation.

annotations.comment: Bromine test rationale is clear, but discuss its selectivity limitations for toluene versus other nonpolar VOCs to deepen evaluation.
The title is concise and directly reflects the investigationâs focus on VOC extraction differences among book ages, providing clear purpose and context.
The research question is precisely stated with measurable variables (mass of VOCs, paper mass, solvent volume, time, book ages), demonstrating excellent focus.
Word count is clearly presented and under the 4000-word limit, fulfilling the prescribed formatting requirement.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-organized, facilitating navigation and demonstrating adherence to IB structure conventions.
Background section effectively links bibliosmia concept to literature (Brunning, 2014; StrliÄ et al. 2009), showing appropriate application of sources.
Health risks of VOCs are noted, but this discussion remains superficial. Consider adding quantitative exposure limits and additional sources for depth.
Figure 1 is well-integrated to illustrate SPME principles, enhancing understanding of non-destructive VOC extraction methods.
Caption for Figure 1 omits source detail. Include full bibliographic reference in caption for clarity and traceability.
Hydrolysis process is clearly described alongside Figure 2, demonstrating strong chemical understanding and effective use of diagrams.
The term âglycosidic bondâ is introduced without mechanistic detail. Provide the reaction equation or mechanism to strengthen conceptual clarity.
Discussion of intermolecular forces (London dispersion, dipoleâdipole) is accurate, linking physical properties of VOCs to volatility.
Introduce the ClausiusâClapeyron equation to quantitatively relate boiling point differences and vapor pressures, improving depth.
The procedure is described in dense paragraphs. Convert the methodology into numbered steps or a flowchart to enhance clarity and reproducibility.
Controlled variables are thoroughly listed, but the text contains a typo in the age list. Correct the description to avoid confusion.
Preliminary trials are documented with visuals, demonstrating thoughtful iterative method development.
Quantitative results from preliminary tests are described qualitatively. Include summary tables or graphs of pretest yields for justification.
Qualitative observations with color changes are well-noted; consider using a spectrophotometer to convert these observations into quantitative absorbance data.
Raw data table (Table 1) is extensive but lacks an immediate caption above the table. Add âTable 1:â in the caption area for proper presentation.
Processed data are clearly summarized in Table 2, showing trend in mass of VOCs with age and standard deviations.
Sample calculation uses population STDEV formula; for small N, apply $$\sigma=\sqrt{\frac{\sum(x_i-\bar x)^2}{N-1}}$$ to reflect experimental uncertainty.
Discussion links RÂ² value to predictive power effectively, demonstrating critical interpretation of statistical metrics.
Evaluation correctly identifies paper composition as limitation; enhance by proposing sourcing same-publisher books to control fiber variables.
The method and safety sections address primary hazards, but mention use of a fume hood or improved ventilation for VOC handling.
Both title and word count meet formatting requirements, demonstrating consistent adherence to IB guidelines.
Method descriptions are occasionally verbose and include redundant details; consider condensing repeated apparatus notes.
Figures 14â16 effectively illustrate color progression of solutions, supporting qualitative statements.
